cells.Inspect Evals (UK AISI eval collection): Zero-config and genuinely viable — PersistBench is the strongest single eval here. Its own scorer already pins a judge provider (persistbench/scorers.py:36-40, DEFAULT_JUDGE_PROVIDER = {'order': ['google-vertex'], 'allow_fallbacks': False}), proving the mechanism, and any target model is pinnable via inspect's `-M provider='{...}'` with no source edit. Amplification is high: the headline metric is an LLM-judged failure_rate compared directly against the paper's 95% bootstrap CI, so judge degradation moves the published conclusion. Held out of the top 3 only on cost/scale — 1300 target generations plus 1300 reasoning-heavy judge calls per arm (~18.7M in / 5.2M out), materially more than the AIME-style options for the same qualitative point. Use --limit to subset if run.

cells.ARC-AGI Benchmarking (ARC Prize Foundati: Zero-config and high-profile. task_utils.py:104-116 merges a gitignored models_private.yml into the model registry, so a pinned-provider model entry is added as pure config with no source edit. Model is deepseek/deepseek-r1-0528, which has a clean 4-endpoint spread including deepinfra/fp4 ($0.50/$2.15) vs novita/fp8 ($0.70/$2.50) and streamlake (logprobs). Amplification is frontier-reasoning plus pass@k variance (num_attempts=2 default) on 120 ARC-AGI-2 public-eval tasks. Main risk is cost variance: max_tokens is configured up to 130,000 and real CoT length is unmeasured, so the estimate spans a >5x range — pilot with a handful of tasks before committing.

cells.CAMEL (Communicative Agents for \"Mind\": Highest raw amplification (9) and NeurIPS 2023, but the zero-config claim is the shakiest in the set and I would not lead with it. model_config_dict is an unvalidated Dict[str, Any] assigned verbatim in base_model.py:279 and deep-copied into each request, so an extra_body.provider does flow through — but the screening agent's own rerun recipe involves writing a driver script that imports examples/ai_society/role_playing.py, which is glue code and therefore outside the principal's constraint. Genuine two-role structure (AI-User vs AI-Assistant) and long multi-turn transcripts that resend full history make it an attractive future target if the no-glue rule is relaxed.
cells.Inspect AI (UK AI Safety Institute): Not a rerun target — it is a framework, not an experiment — but it is the REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION for this whole exercise and the reason several other rows are zero-config. Verified in upstream source: OpenRouterAPI.__init__ collects a `provider` dict model arg and completion_params() emits it as extra_body['provider'], so `-M provider='{"only": ["cerebras/fp16"], "allow_fallbacks": false}'` pins any inspect-based eval with zero code changes. Its own source also documents cross-provider behavioural divergence: a comment notes that AkashML and Parasail reject requests with more than one system message even though the OpenAI API is permissive — corroboration that 'same model, different provider' is not only a quantization story.

cells.openbench (Groq): RECOMMENDED #2 — zero-config, and the cheapest decisive test we found. `bench eval gpt_oss_aime25 --model openrouter/openai/gpt-oss-120b -M provider='{...}'` reaches the wire: openbench's `-M` is yaml.safe_load-parsed (_cli/utils.py:39-59) and forwarded as model_args into inspect_ai's eval(), whose OpenRouterAPI collects a `provider` dict kwarg and emits it as extra_body.provider (verified in inspect_ai/model/_providers/openrouter.py, __init__ + completion_params). NOTE: the `-M only=Cerebras` form is wrong — inspect wants the whole dict under `provider`. Best precision gap available anywhere in the survey: cerebras/fp16 ($0.35/$0.75, 100% uptime, logprobs) vs wandb/fp4 ($0.04/$0.14, 95% uptime, logprobs) — fp16 vs fp4 on the same slug, and both expose logprobs so the divergence can also be read out distributionally. 30 AIME-2025 questions x epochs=8 (hardcoded) = 240 completions per arm; 3 conditions = 720 completions, ~5.8M output tokens, ~$3.20 total, well under 1 h.

cells.MathArena (ETH Zurich SRI Lab): RECOMMENDED #1 — zero-config. configs/models/moonshot/k26.yaml already carries `extra_body: {provider: {allow_fallbacks: False, order: [moonshotai]}}`, splatted verbatim into the OpenAI SDK call at src/matharena/runner.py:293; six sibling configs use the same shape, so pinning is an established in-repo pattern and needs no source edit — copy the YAML and change the `order` list. Notable in its own right: the pinned `moonshotai` endpoint is int4 on OpenRouter, so MathArena's published Kimi-K2.6 leaderboard number is measured on an int4 backend while an fp8 endpoint (streamlake/fp8) of the same slug exists. A/B = moonshotai/int4 ($0.95/$4.00 per Mtok) vs streamlake/fp8 ($0.855/$3.60), both ~100% uptime. Amplification is frontier-reasoning: near-threshold competition math where one flipped token changes the final boxed integer, auto-graded so no judge confound. 30 problems x n=4 x 2 competitions x 3 conditions (strong / weak / strong-replicate for the noise floor) = 720 samples, ~7.2M output tokens, ~$42 total, ~1.9 h at the repo's own concurrent_requests=32.

cells.R1 CoT Illegibility Revisited (nostalgeb: HIGHEST-VALUE EVIDENCE, AND IT IS ALREADY FREE. This is the repo our survey marked SAFE, and its committed runs/ directory already contains the natural experiment: same slug deepseek/deepseek-r1, same GPQA-diamond pipeline, provenance logged per response (src/inference/providers.py:104). Unpinned runs served mostly by Targon score mean illegibility 4.31 +/- 2.13 (n=295) at 36.6% accuracy; provider-pinned Novita runs score 2.31 +/- 0.75 (n=198) at 43.9% — an ~88% swing in the headline metric under one model name. One unpinned run drifted mid-experiment (only 169/300 responses from Targon) and landed at 31.4% accuracy, a 12.5-point spread against a pinned run. Caveat: those runs are 6 months apart, so checkpoint drift is confounded with provider — which is precisely the argument for the paired same-day rerun. Rerunning is zero-config (`openrouter_provider` is a first-class YAML field; the author pins in endpoint-tag form e.g. `targon/fp8`, `deepinfra/bf16`, confirming provider.only accepts slug/quant). Weakness: deepseek/deepseek-r1 now has only 2 live endpoints — azure (ctx 163840, $1.485/$5.94) vs novita/fp8 (ctx 64000, $0.70/$2.50) — a modest, partly-unknown quality gap. ~$15 and ~1 h if rerun; $0 to cite the existing numbers.
